hook: Caffeine doesn't give you energy; it just masks your sleep pressure.

script.hook: Caffeine doesn't give you energy; it just masks your sleep pressure.
beats: Adenosine builds up in your brain the longer you stay awake, creating a biological need for sleep.
When you consume caffeine, it blocks adenosine receptors, preventing you from feeling tired, but it doesn't stop adenosine production.
This leads to a 'caffeine crash' when the effects wear off, revealing the sleep debt you've accumulated.
script.close: Follow BestKarma for science-backed health without the hype.

visualPlan: Visual of a brain with adenosine levels rising over time.
Animation showing caffeine blocking adenosine receptors.
Text overlay with practical tips on caffeine consumption and napping.
